methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $29.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Scale AI, a Series A defense and security company founded by Alexandr Wang and Lucy Guo, is evaluated against a comparative universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Saronic.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the company's last post-money valuation of $29.0B, applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A advancement, yielding $58.0B. Peer-Median analysis draws from three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M. The median across this set is $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M.

The triangulated valuation range spans $255M (low) to $66.7B (high), with a median of $29.1B. The extreme disparity between peer-median and stage-progression results reflects a fundamental tension: the peer set values Series A defense companies substantially below Scale AI's current post-money valuation, suggesting either significant performance divergence from comparable firms or a valuation outlier. Key uncertainties include whether Stage A multiples apply to a company already valued at scale, and whether the peer universe adequately reflects Scale AI's operational metrics or market position.
